zh.lifeSummary: Friswell Hallam於1355年出生在英國。她於1375年與Barnard de Bois在英國艾可頓 加農結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世在英國艾可頓 加農。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Friswell Hallam于1355年出生在英国。她于1375年与Barnard de Bois在英国艾可頓 加農结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世在英国艾可頓 加農。

nameMeanings.name: Hallam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (North Midlands): habitational name from Halam (Nottinghamshire) or from Kirk or West Hallam (Derbyshire), all named with the Old English dative plural halum ‘(at the) nooks or corners of land’ (from Old English halh ‘nook, recess’; see Hale 1). The name may also be derived from Hallam in Sheffield (Yorkshire), sometimes called Hallamshire, named from Old English hallum ‘(at the) rocks’, the dative plural of Old English hall ‘stone, rock’, Old Norse hallr.
English: possibly a variant of Allam with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Hale
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived in a (usually remote) nook or corner of land, from Old English and Middle English hale, dative of h(e)alh ‘nook, hollow’, or a habitational name from a place so named such as Hale in Cheshire, Hampshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, Holme Hale (Norfolk), Hale Street (Kent), and Haile (Cumberland). In northern England the word often has a specialized meaning, denoting a piece of flat alluvial land by the side of a river, typically one deposited in a bend. See Haugh . In southeastern England it often referred to a patch of dry land in a fen. In some cases the surname may be a habitational name from any of several places in England named with this fossilized inflected form, which would originally have been preceded by a preposition, e.g. in the hale or at the hale. This surname is also established in south Wales.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Céile (see McHale ).
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Halle  .

variants.name: Hullum
meanings: English: variant of   Hallam  .

variants.name: Hallum
meanings: English: variant of Hallam , or a variant of Allam with a prosthetic H-.

Norwegian: habitational name from any of the three farmsteads so named in southeastern Norway, from either the dative plural of Old Norse hǫll ‘slope’ or Old Norse Hallheimr, a compound of hallr ‘sloping’ + heimr ‘farmstead’.

variants.name: Hampshire
meanings: English:

(Yorkshire): habitational name from the southern English county so called, which derives its name from Hampton (i.e. the port of Southampton) + Old English scīr ‘division, district’. The County was formerly called Southamptonshire.

(Surrey and Kent): habitational name from the area of Hallamshire in southern Yorkshire, named from Hallam + Middle English schir ‘division’, ‘administrative region’ (Old English scīr). The surname is most common in Yorkshire, where this second derivation is most likely to be the source.

variants.name: Hallums
meanings: English (Essex and Kent): probably a habitational name from Hallams Farm or The Hallams in Wonersh, Surrey, from Middle English Hullehammes ‘hill enclosures’, ‘enclosures (by the) hill’, or, alternatively, a variant of Hallam or Allam , with excrescent -s.

variants.name: Allam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Mohamed, Abdo, Ahmed, Ali, Amani, Ashraf, Azmy, Elsayed, Faysal, Hamdy, Mahmoud, Mamdouh.

English: possibly a habitational name from Alham in Somerset or a variant of Hallam with loss of initial H-, a habitational name from Halam, Nottinghamshire or from Kirk or West Hallam in Derbyshire. Occasionally the surname may be a variant of Allen .
Muslim: from the Arabic personal name ʿAllām ‘very learned’.

variants.name: Allum
meanings: English: possibly a habitational name from Alham in Somerset or a variant of Hallam with loss of initial H-. Post-medieval examples may alternatively represent a variant of Allen .
Muslim: variant of   Allam   or   Alam  .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads in southeastern Norway, probably named from Old Norse Aldheimar, a compound of ald ‘high’ + heimar ‘farm’.

nameMeanings.name: Friswell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: probably a variant of Frizzell (1), reshaped as if from a place-name containing Old English welle, Middle English well ‘well, spring’.

variants.name: Frizzell
meanings: English and Irish: nickname from Old French fresel, frisel ‘lace, ribbon, braid, brooch, frill, ruff’, for someone with an ornate style of dress, or ‘object of little value’ (a diminutive of frese, of ancient Germanic origin).
Scottish: from Anglo-Norman French fresel ‘strawberry’, an alternative form of Fraser .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Friswell Hallam was born in 1355, in England. She married Barnard de Bois in 1375, in Theydon Garnon, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in Theydon Garnon, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Friswell Hallam est née en 1355, à Angleterre. Elle a épousé Barnard de Bois en 1375, à Theydon Garnon, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée à Theydon Garnon, Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Friswell Hallam wurde 1355 in England geboren. Sie hat 1375 in Theydon Garnon, Essex, England Barnard de Bois geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb in Theydon Garnon, Essex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Friswell Hallam è nata il nel 1355 nel England. Lei sposato Barnard de Bois nel 1375, nel Theydon Garnon, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel Theydon Garnon, Essex, England.

ja.Theydon Garnon, Essex, England: イギリスエセックススエイドン ガーノン
ja.lifeSummary: Friswell Hallamは1355年にイギリスで生まれた。1375年にイギリスエセックススエイドン ガーノンでBarnard de Boisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスエセックススエイドン ガーノンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Friswell Hallam(은)는 1355년 영국에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1375년 영국 에세스 테이던 가넌에서 Barnard de Bois(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 에세스 테이던 가넌에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Friswell Hallam nasceu em 1355, em Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Barnard de Bois em 1375, em Theydon Garnon, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em Theydon Garnon, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Friswell Hallam родилась в 1355 г. в местности Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Barnard de Bois в 1375 г. в местности тидон гарнон, эссекс, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в местности тидон гарнон, эссекс, Англия.
